spec_match.rationale: Sets up the right shape (Worker + SharedArrayBuffer + flag spin) but the producer/consumer loop is collapsed into a single worker that writes 1 then immediately writes 0 — so the spin in 'runPlain'/'runPause' almost never iterates because the flag is always 0 on entry. It does not exercise a true cross-thread wait.

interactive.rationale: Handoffs input + run button; gauges and log update with measured worker ms. Cross-origin isolation detection produces a clearly visible warn block.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb says 'a Worker spin-waiting on a flag in SharedArrayBuffer' — but the actual code stores 1 then immediately stores 0 in the same loop, so no other thread ever flips the flag and the spin is degenerate. The framing oversells what the code does.

edge_cases.rationale: Has a non-isolated fallback (good); doesn't show busy-spin where the consumer actually starves the producer; doesn't surface the case where Atomics.pause is absent in the worker (different feature-detection scope from the main thread).

references.rationale: see-also includes chromestatus.com/feature/5106098833719296 and the TC39 proposal.

design_system.rationale: Variables throughout, warn-block uses --accent-rose, no raw hex.

openQuestions.title: Make the spinlock actually cross-thread
openQuestions.detail: Currently a single worker writes 1 then 0 to the same flag, so the consumer's spin never iterates more than once. Spawn two workers — producer and consumer — sharing the SharedArrayBuffer. The consumer should spin on the flag while the producer writes on a separate timer. The pause-vs-no-pause comparison then becomes meaningful.

openQuestions.title: Surface 'how many spin iterations actually happened'
openQuestions.detail: The current gauges show total wall-clock ms but not the number of times the consumer probed the flag while it was empty. That count is the real proof Atomics.pause is doing work — readers can see the spin count drop with the pause hint enabled.

openQuestions.title: Feature-detect Atomics.pause in the worker, not just main
openQuestions.detail: self.crossOriginIsolated is the only branch — but Atomics.pause could be absent in the worker scope too (older browsers). Probe inside the worker and post the result back, then render the same support note as on the main thread.

summary: Right scaffolding (Worker + SharedArrayBuffer + flag) but the central producer/consumer loop is a single-thread self-toggle, so the demo doesn't actually exercise spin-waiting. Splitting into two workers (producer + consumer) and reporting iteration counts is the fix that unlocks this concept.
